While it's probably true that most software engineers seem to be more naturally curious... Are all of them? All of the time? Let's discuss.

We need to seek to understand, look for data points, and work towards our own conclusions. Let's discuss.

There are always a million things to do and pull to go in to different directions, but as engineering leaders we need to be able to reduce the chaos for our teams.

video:video.video:title: It Goes BOTH Ways - Building Trust With Software Engineers
video:video.video:description:
When we talk about leading, we often talk about building trust. This is usually trust that you need to earn from the people you lead.
However, from working with software engineers, I recognize that we must also learn to trust others.
Let's see how that plays out.

video:video.video:title: Leading With Why Over How as a Software Engineer
video:video.video:description:
There's a common pitfall that I see for many software engineers and engineering managers: They lean too hard into the HOW of things and often leave out the WHY.
Seems simple enough, right? Let's discuss the impact.

video:video.video:title: Strategies For Getting Unblocked as a Software Engineer
video:video.video:description:

video:video.video:description:

video:video.video:title: What REALLY Went Wrong with My Vibe Coding Live Stream
video:video.video:description:
So, I ran a live stream to vibe-code the landing page for Code Commute. It didn't go as planned.

Our jobs are safe.

Working... On my own stuff :)

video:video.video:title: REALLY? These AI Agents Are Replacing Software Engineers?

The results? Let's see if my job remains safe...

video:video.video:description:

But Copilot was able to solve it all for me... but only after I gave it one critical piece of information!

video:video.video:title: Toxic Software Dev Culture And Ramping Up The Job Search

A frustrated viewer wrote in to describe some of the challenges they're facing at their current employer. Looks like it's time to move on.
How can they make their job search more effective?

video:video.video:title: Break Into Big Tech - Is The Grass ACTUALLY Greener?
video:video.video:description:
A viewer wrote in to ask about their struggles with breaking into Big Tech as a software engineer. Are they on the cusp of getting it?
Let's discuss!

video:video.video:title: FIRST TIME Leading Software Engineering Projects
video:video.video:description:
A viewer has found that they're in a situation where they'll be leading a project for the first time in a team or technical lead position. However, the other people are their newly hired peers.
How might they navigate this?

video:video.video:title: Just LUCK?! How Did I Get This Far In My Software Developer Interview?
video:video.video:description:
Was it just luck that a viewer found that they could move onto the next interview step? Or is it possible they're demonstrating great qualities of an aspiring junior developer?
Let's discuss!

video:video.video:title: 10 Tips From ChatGPT On Landing A Software Development Role
video:video.video:description: A viewer asked for thoughts on landing a software development role after graduating from bootcamp. I turn to ChatGPT to critique some proposed ideas and offer my suggestions.

video:video.video:title: Final Developer Interview Rounds: Assessing Team Fit
video:video.video:description: A viewer has a unique opportunity where they're going to be on a longer type of internship position and they're in their final interview rounds. What should they prepare for?!

video:video.video:title: Advice For Junior Devs Who Aren't Quitting Because AI
video:video.video:description: A common theme of question that comes in: What advice do I have for junior developers or interns? Let's discuss!

video:video.video:title: Estimating Projects In Software Engineering
video:video.video:description:
The only thing for certain about estimating software engineering projects: the estimates will be wrong.
So... does that mean we should ignore them? Let's discuss!

video:video.video:title: Dealing With Burnout In Software Development
video:video.video:description: Burnout is unfortunately very common in software development. What does it mean to be burnt out, and how can we navigate it?

video:video.video:title: How Do You Do The Job Of A Senior Software Engineer?
video:video.video:description:
A question from Reddit that seemed interesting to discuss: How do you do the job of a senior software engineer?
What's expected and what goes into the role? Let's discuss!

video:video.video:title: AI Is Making Developers Illiterate
video:video.video:description:
AI is the doom of software development!!11oneone Not only is it out there stealing jobs, it's making us lazy at the same time.
Let's discuss.

video:video.video:title: Is Coding At Work The Same As Coding At College Or University?
video:video.video:description: Many of us go to college or university to learn to become software developers. But is there a difference between how we code in school vs how we code on the job?

video:video.video:title: Software Architects: Should They Be The Ones Prototyping?
video:video.video:description:
Should software architects be the ones prototyping for an organization? Maybe!
... But what does this look like? What are the tradeoffs?

video:video.video:title: LeetCode Troubles And Working On Soft Skills As A Developer
video:video.video:description: A viewer wrote in and wanted help navigating challenges with understanding LeetCode as well as getting better at soft skills. What options do they have to consider?

video:video.video:title: HELP! How Should Developer Interns Leverage Their Mentors?
video:video.video:description: A viewer has scored an internship -- awesome work! But how should they approach asking questions on the team, and how do they leverage their mentor?

video:video.video:title: How To Manage Developers - Transitioning From Engineer To Manager
video:video.video:description: Most software engineering managers were once software engineers themselves -- so what did they try and focus on to adjust in their role when they made the transition? Let's discuss!

video:video.video:title: Demonstrating Ownership: Mid-Level To Senior Software Engineer
video:video.video:description: After making a few videos on this and talking through some examples, it was time to update one of my suggested focus areas for moving toward a senior developer. And it's all about ownership!

video:video.video:title: At A Crossroad: Software Development Or Other IT Role Instead?
video:video.video:description: A viewer wrote in to ask about their upcoming dilemma: Do they try to pursue a software development role since they enjoy programming? Or should they stick to the type of non-programmer IT role that they've been enjoying?

video:video.video:title: Waste Of Time For Developers?! Is College Or University Worth It?
video:video.video:description: A viewer wrote in to ask about how to balance their efforts when it comes to learning to be a software developer -- but are college and university even worth it for the course material?

video:video.video:title: DREAM JOB As A Software Engineer But... I'm Stagnating!
video:video.video:description: A software developer seemingly had a dream job BUT... they were worried that they were stagnating in their career. How should they move forward?

video:video.video:title: How To Demonstrate OWNERSHIP As A Software Engineer
video:video.video:description: What happens when you get assigned a totally crappy feature area and it's destined to be a challenge? Step up and take ownership. Let's talk about how.

video:video.video:title: Thief Of Joy: Comparing Yourself With Other Software Engineers
video:video.video:description: A viewer wrote in to describe how they don't feel they're making progress at the same rate as their classmates. As developers, how should we approach this?

video:video.video:title: TOP STRUGGLES Of Junior Developers And Interns
video:video.video:description: A viewer asked for thoughts on common challenges that early-in-career developers face when getting started. I've worked with many, and I'm happy to share some common patterns!

video:video.video:title: The FASTEST Way From Junior To Senior Developer (doesn't exist)
video:video.video:description:
Everyone is looking for efficient paths forward -- so what is the most efficient way to go from a junior developer to a senior developer?
Well, it depends.

video:video.video:title: Zuckerberg On Rogan - Software Engineers OBSOLETE Or Augmented?
video:video.video:description: I did finally watch the Mark Zuckerberg and Joe Rogan podcast episode. Everyone has been spreading fear, but I agree with a lot of what Zuck had to say.

video:video.video:title: AI Advancements: Machine Learning Is DONE FOR! (or is it?)
video:video.video:description: Someone wrote in to ask for advice regarding the start of their career given their focus on machine learning given all of the AI advancements. Are they done for, or is there a bright future ahead?

video:video.video:title: How To Escape TUTORIAL HELL As A Software Developer
video:video.video:description:
Nothin' wrong with reading articles and watching tutorials... Until you find yourself stuck in tutorial hell!
Let's discuss how to get out.
video:video.video:tag: how to get out of tutorial hell

video:video.video:title: Zuckerberg's AI Developer Fear Factory: Following Up On Comments
video:video.video:description: After a long day at the office, I wanted to reflect on some of the comments and themes from my previous video on the Zuckerberg & AI discussions.

video:video.video:title: Tech Stacks, Fundamentals, And LeetCode: What I Look For When Hiring Developers
video:video.video:description: A viewer asked what I look for when hiring senior software engineers! My answer may not reflect every hiring manager's opinion -- but I certainly do have a perspective on it!

video:video.video:title: Am I Ready To Apply For Software Developer Jobs?
video:video.video:description:
A viewer wrote in and said they've been building iOS apps -- but how do they know if they're ready to start applying to jobs?
Let's discuss!

video:video.video:title: Zuckerberg Said NO MORE Software Engineers! PANIC TIME!
video:video.video:description: Our good friend Zuck says AI is about to wipe software engineers from existence. Cool story bro -- let's think about how this works in practice.

video:video.video:title: Most People Don't Need Managers
video:video.video:description: Let's just say as a software engineering manager, I have some thoughts on the benefits of a good manager and the detriment a bad or absent manager can have. But hey, I'm just a nerd in a car, right?

video:video.video:title: Software Engineering Interviews: HOW And WHAT To Focus On
video:video.video:description: LeetCode, system design, and behavioral interviews -- OH MY! What should we prioritize when interviewing as software engineers?

video:video.video:title: Software Engineers On Behavioral Interviews: Individuals Not Teams
video:video.video:description: A good topic from a Reddit thread asked about how to navigate behavioral interviews when software engineers find that they don't collaborate much within their teams. How should they focus on their answers?

video:video.video:title: WHO WINS: Java vs JavaScript vs C# vs...
video:video.video:description: A viewer had asked about my thoughts on C# and DotNet compared to some other languages and tech stacks -- why is it that it doesn't seem as popular? Let's dig into it.

video:video.video:title: NO PROMOTION! Getting Visibility As A Software Engineer
video:video.video:description:
A viewer messaged in to ask about visibility to progress in their career as a software engineer.
How can we go about improving the visibility of the work we're doing?

video:video.video:title: PITFALLS To Avoid: Senior Developer To Engineering Manager
video:video.video:description:
You've landed the promotion from senior engineer to being an engineering manager -- congrats!
... But is it actually a promotion? What do you need to watch out for?

video:video.video:title: What Keeps YOU Motivated As A Developer To Get Better?
video:video.video:description:
Money. Nothin' wrong with that if it's your answer -- but this is something that we should all periodically reflect on.
It might be money one day. It might be your time another day. Let's dive into it.

video:video.video:title: Not A Good Coder But I Want To Make Lots Of Money
video:video.video:description: Generic questions get generic answers, but when this Redditor asked for a roadmap to kick off their software engineering journey, I wanted to explain why asking for an open-ended roadmap isn't a helpful start.

video:video.video:title: How To Conduct A REVERSE Software Engineering Interview
video:video.video:description:
So you're trying to line up that dream job but... How do you make sure it's actually the place you want to work?
What questions should you ask and how do you prepare?

video:video.video:title: Becoming An Irreplaceable Software Engineer
video:video.video:description:
An interesting question from Reddit popped up where the author asked about becoming irreplaceable...
But is this a job security thing, or is the word we want here instead invaluable?

video:video.video:title: Are Promotions BETTER Or WORSE Than The Tech Job Market?
video:video.video:description:
If you were interested in driving your software engineering career forward, does it make sense to focus on promotions or look to the tech job market?
What should we be considering when navigating this?

video:video.video:title: NON-STOP Randomization As A Software Engineer!
video:video.video:description:
It's not uncommon for priorities to change at work for software engineers... But it can suck when getting randomized.
What can we do about that?

video:video.video:title: I Don't Want To Chase Promotions As A Software Engineer
video:video.video:description:
A question from Reddit regarding not wanting to make software development a grinding lifestyle.
Should we tell recruiters this? I have a different idea, but I think it's still worth discussing...

video:video.video:title: They Aren't Promoting Me - Should I Stay Here As A Developer?
video:video.video:description:
A viewer wrote in and asked for an opinion on a situation: Their manager seems to be leading them on about their promotion.
Should they stick around or should they move on?

video:video.video:title: Am I Just Coasting As A Software Engineer?
video:video.video:description:
How might you define coasting for yourself? Is this a shared definition, do you think?
Let's talk about what coasting might mean in software engineering.

video:video.video:title: Increase Your LUCK Surface Area: Learn-in-Public And Networking
video:video.video:description:
A TikTok viewer asked for more details about how to approach learning in public.
How to get people to see? How does that translate into job opportunities?

video:video.video:title: Struggling To Meet Senior Engineer Expectations (Or Is It Just Ramp Up?)
video:video.video:description:
Submitted by a viewer, the goal of this video is to navigate some challenges with a remote hire trying to ramp up into their senior engineering role.
What should they be focusing on?

video:video.video:title: Why Are Software Engineers Online SO ARROGANT?!
video:video.video:description:
Straight from Reddit: What's with all of the software engineers posting online and their level of arrogance?
Let's discuss social media, authority building, and software engineering!

video:video.video:title: What Projects For My Developer Job Application?
video:video.video:description:
Everyone seems to have a different opinion on what projects are best to go on your resume or job application.
Just because they are different, it doesn't mean they are wrong. Let's discuss!

video:video.video:title: DOUBLE The Pay? But Is This Engineering Role Risky?
video:video.video:description:
We always see on social media people sharing their epic stories of huge salary jumps from switching jobs.
So let's switch things up. What does it look like when you're second-guessing the shiny new job offer?

video:video.video:title: Why I Can't Remember ANYTHING From Meetings As A Developer?
video:video.video:description:
We don't like being in meetings in the first place... but what the heck kind of value do they provide if I can't even remember anything from attending?!
Let's discuss some tips!
video:video.video:tag: meetings are a waste of time

video:video.video:title: STOP MICROMANAGING ME! - Navigating Relentless Status Updates
video:video.video:description:
We've probably all faced this at some point as a developer: Is it done yet? Is it done yet? Is it done yet?
How can we navigate situations like this constructively?

video:video.video:title: The Bane Of Every Developer's Existence... MEETINGS!
video:video.video:description: Let's be real here... who actually likes meetings? As developers, they suck. But what can we do about this?
video:video.video:tag: meetings are a waste of time

video:video.video:title: Who Needs Developer Interns Anyway?! (YOU Might...)
video:video.video:description: Internships for software developers can be an incredible opportunity. But what about the impact that they have on the development team?

video:video.video:title: No Copilot?! Bro, DO YOU EVEN Software Engineer?!
video:video.video:description: What sorts of things am I using AI for these days in my software development? I have a few core use cases, but I would love to hear yours too!

video:video.video:title: Do We REALLY Need QA Teams Or Can Developers Just Do It?
video:video.video:description: What does it look like to have a team of dedicated QA vs other variations of making engineering teams responsible for quality? Is there a middle ground? A balance to be struck?

video:video.video:title: Communication Is FAILING Your Development Team
video:video.video:description: Inspired by a topic from Reddit, this video is about a reflection on software engineering teams and where communication can be improved. These are reflections from my own experience -- enjoy!

video:video.video:title: Users DO NOT CARE About Clean Code! (But Maybe You Should)
video:video.video:description: Find me a user who's decided to use a product based on clean code, and I'd love to interview you on my main channel! Until then, it remains something software developers care about -- and probably for good reasons!

video:video.video:title: HELP! My Technical Writing SUCKS As A Software Developer
video:video.video:description: Technical writing (if it isn't already) will be a part of your software engineering career. I can guarantee it! So... How can we do it effectively?

video:video.video:title: This Software Engineer SUCKS With Customers (Or Do They?)
video:video.video:description: A Redditor reports that their manager had to give them negative feedback from a client regarding their attitude... How should software developers approach this?

video:video.video:title: WRONG Software Engineers Are Throwing Me Under The Bus!
video:video.video:description: Have you been in a situation where you're in a meeting and someone tries to belittle you or throw you under the bus? How much worse is it when they're factually incorrect?

video:video.video:title: Switching To The DARK SIDE - Becoming An Engineering Manager
video:video.video:description: A viewer asked at what age and why I chose to become a software engineering manager -- but this might not be the answer that you were expecting.

video:video.video:title: Shut Up And TAKE THE MONEY!! (How To Balance Software Engineering)
video:video.video:description: As a developer, should you leave the happy healthy team in favor of more money and more growth opportunities? Let's see what factors we may want to consider.

video:video.video:title: I'm AFRAID To Ask My Developer Boss Questions...
video:video.video:description: I believe psychological safety is one of THE most important things for ensuring you have effective engineering teams. Let's see how we can navigate scenarios where it seems missing.

video:video.video:title: WHAT TO FOCUS ON?! Starting On A New Dev Team In Big Tech
video:video.video:description: Congrats on starting on a new dev team! But... what's the best way to hit the ground running and be productive? Let's discuss!

video:video.video:title: RISKING IT ALL By Switching From Engineering To Product?
video:video.video:description: A viewer wanted some perspective on switching over to the dark side -- so I figured I'd tell you about one of my best friends and some of his journey.

video:video.video:title: How To MAXIMIZE College As A Software Engineer
video:video.video:description: A viewer commented and said they were headed back to school -- so what's the advice for being able to get the most out of their time while being back at the books?

video:video.video:title: Dealing With Imposter Syndrome As A Younger Tech Lead
video:video.video:description:
So they made you a tech lead, eh? There's only one problem...
You're younger than the other people on the team.
Say hi to your new friend, Imposter Syndrome!

video:video.video:title: Landing Big Tech Roles Without Distributed Systems Experience
video:video.video:description:
Is it possible to work in Big Tech without having any experience working in distributed systems?
Sure -- I did it. So can you. But nobody is saying it's easy.

video:video.video:title: SEND HELP! My Previous Dev Team Won't Leave Me Alone!!
video:video.video:description:
Jumping over to Reddit for this one! How can we navigate situations where we switch teams and the previous team keeps hounding us?
Is this preventable? Let's see!

video:video.video:title: Remote Developers Are All SLACKING!! (Or... Are They?)
video:video.video:description:
Return to office! Everyone, get back to your desk! There's overwhelming evidence that remote devs are all slackers!
But seriously, let's discuss effective remote work.

video:video.video:title: The RIGHT And The WRONG Way To Help Junior Software Engineers
video:video.video:description:
Tell the junior devs the answer and kick 'em away, right?!
Yeah, let's find a more effective way to ensure the junior software engineers get the help they need.

video:video.video:title: Why Are Software Engineering Roles SO COMPETITIVE?!
video:video.video:description:
What's the deal with minimum requirements for job applications? How did the bar start getting set so high?
Let's discuss what's in our control as software engineers.

video:video.video:title: I'm A Software Engineer But I Need Help With Soft Skills
video:video.video:description:
Soft skills are absolutely critical for software engineers -- it's not just about slangin' code.
We get plenty of opportunities to practice technical skills, but what about soft skills?

video:video.video:title: We're AGILE Developers! (And What That ACTUALLY Means)
video:video.video:description:
Agile, with a capital A, is a trigger word for a lot of developers these days.
Let's discuss what agility looks like from my experience in a startup and in Big Tech.

video:video.video:title: YOU'RE FIRED! - Balancing Coaching And Managing Out Developers
video:video.video:description:
There's not a single person who enjoys difficult conversations around letting people go.
Let's discuss some of what goes into developers rebounding vs needing to be let go.

video:video.video:title: BURN OUT: Why Are Software Engineers ALWAYS Suffering From It?
video:video.video:description:
Why is it that software engineering and burn out seem to go hand-in-hand?
Let's discuss common causes for burn out in developers.

video:video.video:title: AI Is Coming For Your Software Engineering Job (Or Is It?)
video:video.video:description:
We all know that software engineering is going to be destroyed by the AI wave.
Orrrr is there a different way that this story goes?

video:video.video:title: I'm DROWNING In Legacy Code! AND There's No Documentation!
video:video.video:description:
A shocker for many new developers: most of your career will be maintaining what we call legacy code.
Sound fun?
Don't worry. It's kickass.

video:video.video:title: Risk It All Or Go The Safe Route? Are Startups Good For Junior Devs?
video:video.video:description:
If you're young and diving into your tech career, should you go the safe route and work for a mid-large size company? Or do you take big risks and go to a startup?
As with all things in software engineering:
It depends.

video:video.video:title: Developers Becoming Influencers And Scammy Course Sellers
video:video.video:description:
Someone was curious why it appears that so many developers who leave tech to become some kind of tech influencer.
What's with the buy-my-course spam and why is it that they didn't just stick with working in tech?

video:video.video:title: The FASTEST Way To Become A Manager As A Developer
video:video.video:description:
As a junior through mid-level engineer, what's the fastest way to become an engineering manager?
First off: Do you actually know if that's what you want?
Let's dive into it.

video:video.video:title: THEY'RE TOO SLOW! - How Long Should It Take Junior Devs?
video:video.video:description:
So just how long should it take a junior dev to ramp up?
You guessed it -- it depends.
Here are my thoughts on things that affect ramp-up time for juniors.

video:video.video:title: Help! I'm A Junior Dev... How Can I Transition To FAANG?
video:video.video:description:
Someone reached out to ask as a junior dev at a small/mid-size company how they can work towards getting into FAANG.
FAANG shouldn't always be an end goal for people, but here are some of my thoughts on what to lean into.

video:video.video:title: A Shortcut To A Software Engineering Role?!
video:video.video:description:
You asked, I answered!
I had someone inquiring about what it might look like to do a role change at a company into a software developer role.
Their focus was more around the different types of projects they might need to focus on -- but I also wanted to highlight some benefits to this approach and some other things to keep in mind.

video:video.video:title: I Keep Getting Rejected! How Do I Get Web Developer Experience?!
video:video.video:description:
You asked, I answered!
This question came in from a developer who was getting feedback that they need more web dev experience before transitioning into these roles.
But how do they get the experience if they can't get a job that gives it to them?

video:video.video:title: What Projects You Should Build As A Junior Developer
video:video.video:description:
I get asked this *all* of the time -- but my answer might not be the same as what other people suggest.
I think there are two major areas to focus on when it comes to building projects as a junior developer: - Which ones are going to keep you interested in learning - Which ones I would care to see on a resume
Spoiler alert: I don't care if you have users for your app. I'm happy for you, truthfully, but that's not the thing I care about when hiring.

video:video.video:title: A Typical Day For An Engineering Manager (Told From Traffic)
video:video.video:description:
You asked -- I answered!
You wanted to know about a typical day in the life of a software engineer, but you ALSO wanted to know what it looks like for an engineering manager.
The reality?
It's all over the damn place.

video:video.video:title: How Engineering Managers Navigate Team Burnout
video:video.video:description:
You asked -- I answered!
On my primary channel, Dev Leader, I had someone asking about a different perspective on burnout.
I have always shared information for individuals and how they can navigate burnout...
But I've never discussed how I try to help my teams navigate burnout as a manager!
video:video.video:tag: day in the life of a software engineer

video:video.video:title: I'm ONLY A Junior Developer - How Can I Work On Leadership?!
video:video.video:description:
This was an awesome question from someone in my Discord community.
Why awesome?
Because it's great to see more junior developers realizing it's not just technical skills they need to focus on.
Better to get started on this stuff early since we get PLENTY of obvious opportunities to work on the technical stuff.
Let's see how you can work on leadership as a junior software engineer.
video:video.video:tag: day in the life of a software engineer

video:video.video:title: A Day In The Life Of A Software Engineer - Startup vs Big Tech
video:video.video:description:
You asked -- I responded!
I was asked about what a day in the life looks like for a software engineer.
While I'm an engineering manager now, I still wanted to share what I've observed and what I've experienced.
The twist: I'll talk about it from my startup and Big Tech experience.
video:video.video:tag: day in the life of a software engineer

video:video.video:title: Software Engineering Is Doom and Gloom - So Watch This!
video:video.video:description:
You asked -- I responded!
Everyone knows software engineering is currently looking like total crap.
There are layoffs. There is AI. There's extreme competition.
Why are we even bothering?
Well -- there's a hell of a lot of awesome stuff to look forward to when you become a software engineer. So stick with it.

video:video.video:title: Exploring Free Tier SaaS Features In BrandGhost
video:video.video:description:
We're giving it all away for free!
Well... not quite. Just the parts that we feel everyone else is already doing.
In this video, I talk about some of the thought process we put into tiering our SaaS product, BrandGhost.

video:video.video:title: My ULTRA Exciting Life Outside Of Software Engineering
video:video.video:description:
Answering your questions: What the heck do I do besides software engineering?
Short answer: Not a whole hell of a lot.
Longer answer?
Watch through and see!

video:video.video:title: YIKES! - Managing a Mis-Leveled Software Engineer (Part 2)
video:video.video:description:
You asked - I responded. And then there was even more information provided, so I responded again!
Big thanks to the viewer who submitted the question and additional information.
This isn't professional consulting, but this is my perspective on how to try and restore a seemingly broken work relationship between an engineering manager and their employee.

video:video.video:title: Who The HELL Needs Documentation?! (*YOU* might...)
video:video.video:description:
You asked - I responded!
What does documentation look like going into greenfield vs existing projects? What kinds of things need to be documented?
What the heck are we supposed to do?!
The reality: it depends.
But you knew that was coming, didn't you?

video:video.video:title: What's the BEST Way to Advance Software Engineering as a College Student?
video:video.video:description:
I get asked this a lot through my primary social accounts and I've talked about this on @DevLeader...
A lot.
I think "Best" is the wrong thing to be asking here because it's not qualified.
Allow me to explain.

video:video.video:title: Migrating My WordPress Blog Over To BLAZOR!
video:video.video:description:
I've been talking about this on my @DevLeader live streams, but I am SO close to getting my blog migrated away from WordPress.
WordPress, we're officially breaking up. I can't stand you. It's not me, it's you.
Blazor is the new hotness, and I am SO ready.

video:video.video:title: Working with DIFFICULT Software Engineers - What To Do?!
video:video.video:description:
So you're working with a software engineer that's a pain in the butt.
To make things worse, they're more senior than you. They've been around for a while.
What should you do in this situation? How should you approach this?

video:video.video:title: Debating On Social Media VS Engineering Conflict Resolution
video:video.video:description:
One will rot your brain. The other will further your career.
One focuses on proving who is right and who is wrong.
The other focuses on a goal moving forward, regardless of who is right and wrong.
Which path will you take?

video:video.video:title: Which Are You: Developer VS Engineer VS PROFESSIONAL Engineer
video:video.video:description:
Get ready for a rant...
It's not that bad, really. Just a recap of a discussion on people making irrational arguments about the fact that "Engineer" is a professional designation in some parts of the world.
But... there were also some helpful things to learn along the way!

video:video.video:title: IT BACKFIRED! Saving Dates And Times As UTC Wrecked Me
video:video.video:description:
60% of the time it works every time.
Saving timestamps anchored to UTC has been the way to go with literally everything I've seen in my career.
... until now.
This was a fun bug to think through, but more fun because real life production code isn't always that trivial.

video:video.video:title: HELP! Software Engineers Stagnating In Their Career Progress
video:video.video:description:
A viewer wanted to know about my thoughts on navigating a challenging situation...
How can we approach a situation where a software engineer seems to be stalled in their role -- they're focused on things that aren't a priority but they're also getting positive reinforcement from other stakeholders on this.
In this vlog, I talk about some of my thoughts navigating this.

video:video.video:title: Answering Your Questions: Technical AND People Centric Roles in Tech
video:video.video:description:
You asked... I answer!
In this vlog, I discuss some different options for tech careers that involve people.
And a big surprise -- most of them involve people. Even software engineering is a field HEAVILY focused on working with people.
Working directly with customers is a different story though. What's that look like?

video:video.video:title: SH*T HIT THE FAN! - How Azure Almost Ended My Hawaiian Vacation
video:video.video:description:
Disaster struck Friday night when I was writing my @DevLeader newsletter.
I received an email from Azure that said our subscription had been disabled.
6 hours of timezone difference between me and the team, how would we get this back online and save our @BrandGhostAI customers from impact?

video:video.video:title: How My Trip To Hawaii Helped With Idempotent Consumers!
video:video.video:description:
Is it still a commute if it's a one-off destination that happens to be not work-related? And if the purpose is instead vacation and the destination is Hawaii?
Maybe not.
But here I am to talk to you about some automatic Azure-scaling mishaps and how I needed to navigate an architectural fix while on vacation.

video:video.video:title: SQL Woes And Why Software Estimates Are (Almost) Always Wrong
video:video.video:description:
Want to hear a fun story about working with a small SQL change?
Fun for you -- not for me. Prematurely optimizing because my environment was misleading me!
Hear all about it and why we should use estimates to trigger conversations to get back on track in this video.

video:video.video:title: Understanding User Feedback Without Blindly Following User Solutions
video:video.video:description:
Many software engineers go through their careers rarely (or never) directly dealing with user feedback.
It's something that some roles can end up sheltering engineers from depending on the organization -- but there's a lot to learn from dealing with users!
In this video, I explain why user feedback is awesome, but why we want to understand their challenges vs their solutions.

video:video.video:title: Do Software Engineers REALLY Need So Much Self-Help?
video:video.video:description:
In response to a LinkedIn comment, I wanted to raise some awareness about self-help topics for software engineers.
No, I don't think this is even specific to software engineering, but this is the field that I work in professionally.
Yes, I do think most people have challenges, insecurities, or other things going on under the surface that have nothing to do with their technical assignments at work.
No, I don't think this makes them weak. The fact they are talking about it and working on it makes them the opposite.

video:video.video:title: What's the REAL Cost of Blame Culture in Software Engineering
video:video.video:description:
Inspired by my @DevLeader newsletter article and livestream discussion, I wanted to talk about how blame culture can have a terrible impact on software engineering teams.
The alternative?
Adopt radical accountability.

video:video.video:title: AMG GT vs Turbo Widebody Audi TT RS: Which One Reigns Supreme?
video:video.video:description:
Not actually commuting today but wanted to get a quick video of the two cars in the driveway while the wife has the X5 out. The AMG was cleaned up after many months of neglect -- a sign that I am over-extending myself when I'm unable to keep up with the little things.
Sometimes washing a car can seem like climbing a mountain when you don't have any capacity left.

video:video.video:title: Hiring Software Engineers And Why I DON'T Set You Up To Fail
video:video.video:description: In this video, I share some perspective on some LinkedIn comments regarding red flags and behavioral interviews. I thought it was a great discussion between two individuals, and I wanted to share my perspective on the matter as well.

video:video.video:title: How And Why To Test Software (And All The Reasons That We Didn't)
video:video.video:description: In this update, I share our path forward on coded tests in @BrandGhostAI -- along with why it's taken us to this point to start writing more tests.

video:video.video:title: I've Been Assigned A BIG Project And I'm SCARED
video:video.video:description:
Hectic start to the morning after a brutal workout -- but we're doin' it!
Quick updates this morning with a big work project, @BrandGhostAI traffic from a newsletter, and our upcoming trip to Maui!

video:video.video:title: Corporate Hot Potato Is Destroying Your Organization
video:video.video:description:
Most of us have probably seen this at work at some point:
"That's not our team's responsibility" or... "Go talk to team X, we don't own that"
The problem is when everyone starts to do this and there's not really forward progress on getting sh*t done. So... what can we do about that?

video:video.video:title: How To Transform User Issues Into A Positive Experience
video:video.video:description:
I'm very fortunate to be working with some awesome folks on @BrandGhostAI.
No doubt about it, we have an awesome team that I wouldn't trade for anything.
In this video, I talk about how we approach things that don't scale as well as being able to have a ton of trust in your team members.

video:video.video:title: DTO? You're Probably Not Taking Enough Time Off.
video:video.video:description:
Morning update as I wrap up CrossFit and reflecting on the on-call shift from the weekend.
I need more rest.
And I need to lead by example here to take the morning off because I'm truly not recovered from a sleep perspective.

video:video.video:title: I Survived CrossFit, But Can I Survive This Zapier Dumpster Fire?
video:video.video:description:
Finally had a good CrossFit workout!
We've been iterating rapidly on @BrandGhostAI feature requests and fixes -- and this is the exact kind of feedback we want!
I have never been so motivated to get BrandGhost working to move away from Zapier for good. I can't wait to never give them another cent.
Let's go!

video:video.video:title: Burnout, Imposter Syndrome, and Soft Skills For Developers
video:video.video:description:
Had to scrap one of the vlogs from this morning due to too much rage -- but let's change gears!
Burnout? Imposter syndrome? What other things plague software developers throughout their careers?
Let's talk!

video:video.video:title: STOP The Gatekeeping and Become a Developer in 2024
video:video.video:description:
On my primary channel, @DevLeader, I published a video about being "too old" to get into development. By the way... what's "too old" supposed to even mean?
It was great to see even more people across social platforms jump in and share their success stories.
And no, AI isn't taking our jobs. Not yet.

video:video.video:title: Our SaaS Feature Development Strategy Revealed: Staggering
video:video.video:description:
As we build out @BrandGhostAI, we're looking to build functionality AND get commits from users to onboard to our service.
How can we prioritize all of the asks?
We prototype backend functionality first -- testing it out as expert users to prove viability.
If our customers want it, we harden it and put in place a rock-solid UX for them!

video:video.video:title: Why We Need to NAIL Onboarding For Our SaaS
video:video.video:description:
Onboarding is absolutely critical for us with @BrandGhostAI -- we take it very seriously.
During this trip to the doctor's office, I share with you how and why we're prioritizing different aspects of customer onboarding to our SaaS!

video:video.video:title: I'M BALD! Here's What Internet Trolls Taught Me About B2B Sales
video:video.video:description: Not a ton to report on except for dealing with trolls on the Internet and my head is now as smooth as an egg.

video:video.video:title: @BrandGhostAI's Hidden Advantage to Faster Releases
video:video.video:description:
You've probably heard someone say something like, "Writing tests now will speed you up later".
We're currently living that with @BrandGhostAI.
We've been fortunate enough that many of our features are light weight and decoupled -- we can iterate fast without risking breaking things.
However, as we onboard users and listen to feedback, we'll need to shift gears.
Enter xUnit and Testcontainers.

video:video.video:title: STOP Ignoring Early SaaS User Feedback - But Don't Follow Blindly!
video:video.video:description:
Feedback is hard for many people -- especially when it's critical.
But you know what's worse than no feedback?
Being stuck with no users because you can't delight your customers.
In this vlog, I chat through how we need to sift through that feedback from our early users.

video:video.video:title: Breaking The Rules - SaaS User Acquisition & Focused Personas
video:video.video:description:
As a software engineer, getting customers isn't easy.
BrandGhost is at an exciting time where we're having customers onboard and exploring their use cases -- and it's important that we understand where we focus!
In this vlog, I discuss some different perspectives around this.

video:video.video:title: Plugins Help Me CRUSH The Rule Of 3 - Here's How!
video:video.video:description:
The rule of three -- Have you heard of it?
In this vlog, I talk about refactoring some BrandGhost code and how I have WAY more than three examples to reflect on.
I had to weigh on a design decision I wasn't big on, but I had over TEN examples that demonstrated it would be very valuable.

video:video.video:title: Do Things That Don't Scale - A BrandGhost Story
video:video.video:description: YCombinator folks have made it clear that there are things founders can and should be doing that simply don't scale in the beginning. For our SaaS, BrandGhost, we've absolutely had to do some things that don't scale as part of where we're at in our growth.

video:video.video:title: How NOT To Network, Get Referrals, And Have Your Resume Reviewed
video:video.video:description:
The number of messages I get from complete strangers asking me to review their resume or give them a referral to where I work is pretty remarkable.
Personally, I refuse to refer unless I've worked with someone. But I literally don't have enough time for MYSELF -- and asking me to do this kind of stuff for you (as a stranger) is kind of bonkers to me.
Here's what I recommend you do instead.

video:video.video:title: Finished Hell Week - Back To The GRIND! (And CrossFit Killed Me)
video:video.video:description: We're back! CrossFit kicked my BUTT today, but I'm all done my on-call shift. Here are some updates to catch you up and we'll get into more software engineering topics later in the day!

video:video.video:title: Meeting Up With Jade Wilson And BrandGhost Coding Updates
video:video.video:description: Great opportunity to finally meet up with Jade Wilson this weekend and create some content together! In this drive into Bellevue, I discuss some blob storage challenges and migrating a live system to use resource identifiers from URLs.

video:video.video:title: Eating Our Own Dog Food With BrandGhost
video:video.video:description: One of the best ways to build software is to eat your own dog food. Not only is it high in protein, but you can truly understand the usage AND catch issues earlier.

video:video.video:title: How I Approach One on Ones as a Software Engineering Manager
video:video.video:description: Everyone has their own opinions about one on ones. Here's how I approach them as a software engineering manager -- regardless of startup of Big Tech.

video:video.video:title: Caffeine And Narcolepsy - My Journey As A Software Engineer
video:video.video:description:
Caffeine is awesome -- but even as someone who loves consuming caffeine, it's completely changed my life.
Sometimes not for the best, unfortunately.

video:video.video:title: Thoughts On BrandGhost Migration To Blob Storage
video:video.video:description: Another rainy drive into the office! On this drive, I discuss my thoughts on shifting from a URL-centric content repository to something like Azure Blob Storage where we can manage the content for BrandGhost users.

video:video.video:title: Chinese Food, Cookies, And Cross Functional Team Challenges
video:video.video:description:
Just another day commuting back from Microsoft!
Had another great one with folks at the office -- but NOT excited to hit a wall of traffic on the way home. Typical, right?
In this drive home, I talk about how to navigate multi-team projects and trying to take a bias for action.

video:video.video:title: This Is What PEAK Productivity Looks Like! (Well, Not Really)
video:video.video:description:
Apparently rolling a camera in the car is harder work than it seems!
On the drive home from work, I discuss course creation for Dometrain as well as how we're focused on being data-driven while building BrandGhost.

video:video.video:title: This Took THREE Takes! But At Least I Got Gas...
video:video.video:description:
Apparently rolling a camera in the car is harder work than it seems!
On the drive home from work, I discuss course creation for Dometrain as well as how we're focused on being data-driven while building BrandGhost.

video:video.video:title: Office Visitors And I'm Low On Gas
video:video.video:description:
Finding out you're low on gas as you merge onto the highway isn't a great start to a Monday.
But that's okay! We have visitors this week coming into the Redmond headquarters and it's great to connect with my @Microsoft365 colleagues!

video:video.video:title: Surprise! Engineering Managers Don't Get Trained.
video:video.video:description:
Top of mind for me this morning is the idea that many ICs get promoted to a software engineering manager role because they are GREAT ICs...
But are they actually great managers? Are they good with people?
Most of us start off this way as managers and we have no idea what the heck we're doing.

video:video.video:title: Engineering Surveys And Audi TT RS Reveal!
video:video.video:description: Another awesome day at the office! Check out the end for a little peek of the Audi TT RS!

video:video.video:title: Podcast Power Failure And Actors As Software Engineers
video:video.video:description: Had an awesome morning podcasting BUT also had a hiccup with a power failure. Hang out with me on my drive to work!

video:video.video:title: I Suck At CrossFit And What That Taught Me About B2B Sales
video:video.video:description:
It turns out I suck at CrossFit.
Like -- really bad.
But that's cool. And in fact, I'm not even that concerned about it. I've been going to the gym for 20+ years and I know how to lift weights, how to manage my nutrition etc...
But I don't have the mobility (yet). I don't have the cardio (yet). I don't have some of the technique (yet).
Keyword: yet.
And this is the same in your software engineering career -- you'll always be in new situations where you don't YET have the skill and experience.
So buckle up.

video:video.video:title: Balancing Time As A Software Engineer And Navigating Hustle Culture
video:video.video:description:
I keep a full plate.
Like... Too full.
Fortunately, I have an amazing wife and she helps me keep my head on straight.
I think that being busy comes in "seasons" but there are certain types of endeavors where the seasons last... indefinitely.

video:video.video:title: Making Tech Interviews Less Awful
video:video.video:description:
I hate Big Tech interviews.
And I don't blame anyone for them -- I think they probably evolved over time to be where they're at.
I think we should be focusing more on maximizing how much value we can gauge from a candidate and NOT trying to setup traps for them to fail along the way.
That means no trick optimizations to pass your coding test, for example.

video:video.video:title: Return To Office - Pros and Cons of Remote vs Office Work
video:video.video:description:
Cars, code, and... traffic.
Welcome to the start of a vlog series where I share my commute time with you talking about software engineering and life.
Today's focus is on in-office vs remote work.
Pros. Cons. Everything in between.
